sourceMovie.title: Little Children
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Little Children (2006) as a drama theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The drama and romance blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Revolutionary Road
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Little Children clicked for you, Revolutionary Road (2008) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and romance, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Manhattan
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Little Children clicked for you, Manhattan (1979) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and romance, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Little Children?
faq.answer: Try Revolutionary Road, Cruel Intentions, Manhattan—each shares DNA with Little Children while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Little Children?
faq.answer: Little Children (2006) is best described as Drama, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Little Children worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.1/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
